objections.location: Step 7 — Code Patterns (Reference code exemplar — Hero)
objections.description: The hero code example claims to be 'raw Tailwind + React that works with zero additional dependencies' but uses shadcn/ui semantic color tokens (bg-background, text-foreground, text-muted-foreground, bg-card, text-card-foreground, ring-border) that do NOT exist in vanilla Tailwind. This code will fail without shadcn setup, misleading developers.
objections.suggested_fix: Either: (1) Use actual Tailwind color utilities (bg-white, text-slate-900, text-slate-500) for zero-dependency examples, OR (2) Explicitly label examples as 'shadcn/ui semantic tokens' and provide the tailwind.config.js setup required to define these colors. Add a third example showing true vanilla Tailwind.

objections.description: The CSS-only column shows `animate-in fade-in slide-in-from-bottom-4` as a Tailwind class, but these are from the tailwindcss-animate plugin, NOT vanilla Tailwind. This directly contradicts the 'CSS-only (Tailwind)' label and the claim of zero dependencies.
objections.suggested_fix: Replace with actual vanilla CSS/Tailwind: `animate-[fadeSlideUp_0.3s_ease-out]` with a custom @keyframes definition, OR clearly note that tailwindcss-animate plugin is required and remove it from the 'CSS-only' column. Add a true CSS-only row using opacity/transform with utility classes.

objections.description: Step 1 says to ask the user for clarification if input is vague, but Step 2 immediately proceeds to gather repo context without a clear pause point. The agent will barrel forward without waiting for user response, breaking the intended flow.
objections.suggested_fix: Add explicit halt instruction: 'If Step 1 requires user clarification, STOP and ask before proceeding to Step 2. Do not gather context until the task scope is clear.'

objections.description: The MCP tool calls are defined with parameter names but no guidance on handling failures. What if recommend_ui_sources returns empty? What if plan_ui_execution errors? The skill has no fallback logic.
objections.suggested_fix: Add explicit error handling: 'If MCP tool call fails or returns empty results, log the issue and fall back to built-in pattern knowledge. Do not halt the process—proceed with Step 4 using internal patterns.'

objections.description: Several essential patterns have decision trees but NO implementation code: Navigation (sticky, transparent-on-scroll, mobile drawer), CTAs, Forms, Pricing, Empty states, Loading states, Error states, Toasts/notifications. The skill describes what to use but not how to build it.
objections.suggested_fix: Add at minimum: (1) Sticky nav with transparent-on-scroll code, (2) Toast notification with z-index, (3) Loading skeleton, (4) Form with inline validation. These are high-frequency needs that shouldn't require external lookup.

objections.location: Step 4 — Color & Dark Mode section vs Step 7 code examples
objections.description: Step 4 defines semantic colors as `--color-surface`, `--color-text-primary`, `--color-text-muted`, `--color-border`, but Step 7 code uses `bg-background`, `text-foreground`, `text-muted-foreground`, `bg-card`. The naming convention is inconsistent within the skill itself.
objections.suggested_fix: Standardize on ONE naming convention throughout. Either use Tailwind CSS v4 syntax (--color-*) or shadcn/ui convention (background, foreground), but not both. Update all references to match.

objections.description: The accessibility principles are comprehensive but missing concrete implementation code. Developers need to see: skip-to-content link implementation, focus-visible ring in action, and aria-live region setup.
objections.suggested_fix: Add a compact 'Accessibility Quick-Start' code block with: (1) Skip link component, (2) Focus-visible button example, (3) Toast with aria-live. These are copy-paste ready and reinforce the non-negotiable stance.

suggestions.description: Add a 'Required Setup' section showing the minimal tailwind.config.js content needed for semantic colors, zIndex scale, and dark mode—so developers can actually run the code examples.
Add a 'Component File Structure' suggestion (e.g., components/ui/, components/sections/, lib/utils.ts for cn helper) so developers know where to place generated code.
Add concrete Web Vitals measurement guidance for Vite: `vite-plugin-web-vitals` or `web-vitals` npm package integration example.
For 'Modal stacking' anti-pattern, add the alternative: 'Use a stepper within a single modal, or redesign as a multi-page flow with progress persistence.'
